claim_text: The patient reports numbness and tingling in the right hand.
The numbness and tingling in the right hand started 6 months ago.
The numbness and tingling in the right hand has become more persistent over the past month.
The numbness and tingling occurs primarily with typing on the keyboard.
The pain is worse when typing.
The pain is relieved with resting.
The pain is relieved with using ibuprofen.
The patient reports pain on extension of the wrist.
The patient reports pain on flexion of the wrist.
The patient reports right-sided numbness and tingling.
The patient reports right-sided wrist pain.
The patient is taking ibuprofen.
A positive Tinel's sign is felt on the inside of the patient's wrist.
There is tenderness on the wrist with extension.
There is tenderness on the wrist with flexion.
The patient has been diagnosed with Carpal Tunnel Syndrome.
A wrist splint is to be worn at night.
The patient will continue taking Ibuprofen.
An outpatient nerve conduction study will be ordered.
The nerve conduction study will evaluate the severity of the condition and the need for treatment.
The patient will be seen for follow-up in 1 week.
The results of the nerve conduction study will be reviewed at the follow-up appointment.
A referral for physical therapy will be considered if the nerve conduction study result is positive.
Surgical options will be discussed further if needed, considering severity and response to conservative treatment.
